metadata.name: Gitlab-Runner
metadata.description: CI/CD build runner for GitLab pipelines. Executes jobs defined in .gitlab-ci.yml using the Docker executor.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
CI/CD build runner for GitLab pipelines. Executes jobs defined in .gitlab-ci.yml using the Docker executor.

## Prerequisites
- Create the runner in GitLab first and copy the runner authentication token before first start.
- Confirm the GitLab instance URL is reachable from the Docker host.
- Decide which default Docker image the runner should use for jobs that do not specify their own image.

## Deploy
- Render `compose.yaml` and `config.toml`, review the runner URL and token, then start the stack with `docker compose up -d`.
- Confirm the runner container stays healthy and check `docker compose logs -f` until it reports as online.

## Post-Install
- Verify the runner appears online in GitLab and execute a small test pipeline before assigning production jobs to it.
- If you need Docker Hub rate-limit relief, replace the example `DOCKER_AUTH_CONFIG` block with your own credentials.

## Docs
- [GitLab Runner](https://docs.gitlab.com/runner/)
- [Register a Runner](https://docs.gitlab.com/runner/register/)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/gitlab/gitlab-runner)

items.name: container_name
items.title: Container Name
items.description: Name to use for the container.

items.name: gitlab_url
items.title: GitLab URL
items.description: Base URL of the GitLab instance.

items.name: gitlab_runner_token
items.title: Runner Authentication Token
items.description: Runner authentication token created in GitLab.

items.name: gitlab_runner_concurrent
items.title: Concurrent Jobs
items.description: Maximum number of jobs the runner can process at the same time.

items.name: gitlab_executor_image
items.title: Default Executor Image
items.description: Default Docker image used for jobs that do not specify one.
